Great! A well-worded no, followed by an exact number, that indicates you've gone to your last penny. You countered here because the longer time frame for a response indicates some frustration on their part, which translates into a good time to make a counteroffer. You ended on a positive note. The last impression is the lasting impression. Well done. The seller accepted your offer of $168,353 and half of the closing costs. Congratulations! You succeeded! Excellent use of personalization, the specific numbers, and employing the empathy building methods of saying no without using the word. This bargaining method would be less effective if there were other potential buyers for this house. Regardless, the letter writing is not only a powerful way to personalize yourself, it's also the only way you can be certain your words reach the other side in the exact form you've structured them. Personalization of yourself and always ending your communications on a positive note are two of the subtle, yet powerful emotional intelligence strategies to guarantee execution. Well done!
